installMethodsSupported.methodDescription:
Recommended Method
Direct Flash the target device from this host machine.
Requirement:
No extra USB stick required.
No monitor for target device required.

Recommended Method
Use the ISO image to create a bootable usb stick. And then use the bootable usb stick to flash the target device.
Requirement:
Extra USB stick required.
Monitor for target device required.

Not a Recommended Method
Use the ISO image to create a bootable usb stick. And then use the bootable usb stick to flash the target device.
Requirement:
Extra USB stick required.
No monitor for target device required.
Note:
This process might be complicated, and requires many manual steps. Use this workaround only if:
1. Direct flash is not working correctly for you.
2. You don't have a monitor to connect to the target device, and want to use the headless installation with the installation USB stick.
3. You want to go into the UEFI setup menu in the headless way.
Install the SDK components on your Jetson device.
Upgrade the Jetson Linux OS to BSP Version 39.2 and update the installed SDK software stack on your Jetson device.

releaseNotes.releaseNotesTooltip:
What's new in JetPack 7.2:
  • Adds Jetson Orin family support in JetPack 7.
  • Enables single-command NemoClaw installation on developer kits.
  • Adds MAXN_SUPER support for Jetson AGX Orin 32GB Super Mode.
  • Enables Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) support on Jetson Thor T5000.
  • Introduces unified ISO-based installation for Jetson Orin and Thor developer kits.
  • Provides official Yocto recipes for Jetson Orin and Thor via the OE4T GitHub repository.
Open Release Notes

groups.name: CUDA
groups.description: The CUDA Toolkit provides a comprehensive development environment for C and C++ developers building GPU-accelerated applications.

groups.name: NvSci
groups.description: NvStreams is a streaming technology which provides a way for libraries understanding high dimensional data (CUDA, VPI etc) to interop with each other.

sudo apt-key del $(apt-key list | grep cudatools -B 1| grep 'pub' | cut -d ' ' -f 4 | cut -d/ -f 2)

NV_L4T_DOCKER_SBSA_HOST_COMP.name: NVIDIA Container Runtime with Docker integration

commands: echo 'for pkg in docker.io docker-doc docker-compose docker-compose-v2 podman-docker containerd runc; do sudo apt-get remove -qq -y $pkg || true; done'
for pkg in docker.io docker-doc docker-compose docker-compose-v2 podman-docker containerd runc; do sudo apt-get remove -qq -y $pkg || true; done
echo 'for pkg in docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin docker-ce-rootless-extras; do sudo apt-get remove -qq -y $pkg || true; done'
for pkg in docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin docker-ce-rootless-extras; do sudo apt-get remove -qq -y $pkg || true; done
# Add the repository to Apt sources:
safe_apt_install "sudo apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin" 5
echo "Purging Docker Engine and related packages..."
(safe_apt_install "sudo apt-get purge -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin docker-ce-rootless-extras" 5) || true
(safe_apt_install "sudo apt-get purge -y docker.io docker-doc docker-compose docker-compose-v2 podman-docker containerd runc" 5) || true
echo "Removing Docker data and configuration (this deletes all images/containers/volumes)..."
echo "Removing Docker APT repo and key..."
echo "Could not find the apt source list file: $LIST_FILE"
safe_apt_install "sudo DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get upgrade -y -o Dpkg::Options::=--force-confnew -o Dpkg::Options::=--force-confdef" 5
echo sleep 30 seconds to make sure Jetson board is connected back after pre check...
echo sleep 10 seconds to make sure device is stable...
# Function to exit with an error code
Write-Output "Windows version is compatible for WSL."
Exit-WithError "Windows version is not compatible for WSL. Please update your Windows."
Exit-WithError "System architecture is not 64-bit. WSL requires a 64-bit processor."
# Function to check if a Windows feature is enabled
# Function to enable a Windows feature
Write-Host "WSL distribution '$distroName' not found. Nothing to unregister."
netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="SDKM JP ADB Inbound"; $true
netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="SDKM JP ADB Outbound"; $true
netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="SDKM JP ADB Inbound" dir=in action=allow program="{{TARGET_ROOT}}\{{BUNDLE_TITLE}}\ADB\adb.exe" enable=yes
netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="SDKM JP ADB Outbound" dir=out action=allow program="{{TARGET_ROOT}}\{{BUNDLE_TITLE}}\ADB\adb.exe" enable=yes
echo sleep 30 seconds to make sure Jetson board is connected back after pre check...
echo sleep 10 seconds to make sure device is stable...
Start-Job -ScriptBlock { usbipd.exe detach --busid (usbipd.exe list | Where-Object { $_ -match "0955:7020" } | ForEach-Object { ($_ -split '\s+')[0] }) }
netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="SDKM JP ADB Inbound"
netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="SDKM JP ADB Outbound"

echo sleep 30 seconds to make sure Jetson board is connected back after pre check.
Start-Job -ScriptBlock { usbipd.exe detach --busid (usbipd.exe list | Where-Object { $_ -match "0955:7020" } | ForEach-Object { ($_ -split '\s+')[0] }) }

content.modalTitle: Flash the Jetson Target Device with Bootable USB Stick

attributes.text: ISO Image Verison: {{COMPONENT_VERSION}}
Please follow the ISO Flashing Guide here to flash the Jetson Target Device with your Bootable USB Stick.
Once done. Click OK to continue.
ISO Image Verison: {{COMPONENT_VERSION}}
1. Plug your USB flash drive (16GB or larger) on the host machine.

2. Use Etcher to create a bootable usb drive:
a) Once it started, select the downloaded ISO image file, select the USB stick you want to write the ISO image to, then click the flash button to create your bootable USB stick.
b) The downloaded ISO image file is saved at {{TARGET_ROOT}}/{{BUNDLE_TITLE}}_{{BOARD_ID}}/ISO_Image
Tip: You can copy the upper directory path and paste it into the Etcher pop-up dialog when selecting the ISO image file.
Note: If you see a "Something went wrong" messgae in Etcher during the verification stage, you can safely ignore it.
Once the bootable usb drive created. Click OK to continue.
ISO Image Verison: {{COMPONENT_VERSION}}
1. Plug your USB flash drive (16GB or larger) on the host machine.

2. Use Etcher to create a bootable usb drive:
a) Once it started, select the downloaded ISO image file, select the USB stick you want to write the ISO image to, then click the flash button to create your bootable USB stick.
b) The downloaded ISO image file is saved at {{TARGET_ROOT}}\{{BUNDLE_TITLE}}_{{BOARD_ID}}\ISO_Image
Tip: You can copy the upper directory path and paste it into the Etcher pop-up dialog when selecting the ISO image file.
Note: If you see a "Something went wrong" messgae in Etcher during the verification stage, you can safely ignore it.
Once the bootable usb drive created. Click OK to continue.

content.modalTitle: Flash the Jetson Target Device with Bootable USB Stick (Headless Mode)

attributes.text: ISO Image Verison: {{COMPONENT_VERSION}}

1. If you are flashing a device that was previously used or flashed (NOT brand new from the factory):
a. Please check the UEFI Firmware & Jetson ISO Compatibility page here to see if your setup requires enabling Display Handoff mode before USB installation.
b. If Display Handoff mode required, please follow the Re-enable USB Stick Installation Guide here to re-enable first.

2. Please follow the ISO Flashing Guide here to flash the Jetson Target Device with your Bootable USB Stick.
Once done. Click OK to continue.

content.modalTitle: Flash the Jetson Target Device with Bootable USB Stick (Headless Mode)

attributes.text: ISO Image Verison: {{COMPONENT_VERSION}}
Please follow the ISO Flashing Guide here to flash the Jetson Target Device with your Bootable USB Stick.
Once done. Click OK to continue.

To enable the Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) feature, your computer must be restarted..
Please save your work and close any open applications before continuing.
The target device has been flashed successfully. To complete the process, please manually reboot (power off and then power on) your target device, and then click "OK" to continue.

content.modalTitle: Flash the Jetson Target Device with Bootable USB Stick

attributes.text: ISO Image Verison: {{COMPONENT_VERSION}}

1. If you are flashing a device that was previously used or flashed (NOT brand new from the factory):

content.modalTitle: Prepare the Jetson Target Device for Headless ISO Flashing

attributes.text: ISO Image Verison: {{COMPONENT_VERSION}}

1. Connect the power supply to the USB‑C port located farther from the HDMI port (the left USB‑C port shown in the image ports (5)).
2. Make sure the device is powered off.
3. Connect a USB cable from your laptop or PC to the Jetson AGX Thor Developer Kit’s Debug-USB port (8) behind the lid cover.
4. Attach the bootable installation USB stick (that you created using Etcher) either on one of the USB Type-A port (1) or on the remaining USB-C port (5) of Jetson AGX Thor Developer Kit.
Once done, click OK to continue.

Upgrade completed successfully.
A reboot is required for all changes to take effect. We recommend rebooting now.

content.modalTitle: Flash the Jetson Target Device with Bootable USB Stick (Headless Mode)

attributes.text: ISO Image Verison: {{COMPONENT_VERSION}}

b. If Display Handoff mode required, please follow the Re-enable USB Stick Installation Guide here to re-enable first.

2. Please follow the ISO Flashing Guide here to flash the Jetson Target Device with your Bootable USB Stick.

SDK Manager could not install WSL automatically on your Windows system.
Please download the latest WSL installer (MSI) from the official WSL releases page on GitHub:
https://github.com/microsoft/wsl/releases
Run the MSI to install WSL manually, then reboot your host machine and restart SDK Manager.

execBash: Write-Host 'Virtualization is ENABLED in BIOS (hypervisor is running, requirement fields unavailable).'
Write-Host 'Unable to determine virtualization status. Please check your BIOS settings manually.'

attributes.text: Could not detect virtualization enabled in the system BIOS. WSL installation requires CPU virtualization to be enabled. Please make sure your computer’s virtualization (Intel VT-x or AMD-V) is enabled in BIOS settings before continuing.

content.modalTitle: Prepare the Jetson Target Device for Headless ISO Flashing

attributes.text: ISO Image Version: {{COMPONENT_VERSION}}

1. Connect the power supply to the Jetson Orin target device.
2. Make sure the device is powered off.
3. Connect a USB cable from your laptop or PC to the USB port used for flashing or device communication on the Jetson Orin target device.
4. Attach the bootable installation USB stick that you created using Etcher to an available USB port on the Jetson Orin target device.
Once done, click OK to continue.

attributes.text:
Unable to access Docker's official apt repository or GPG key.
Official Docker apt repository: https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu
Please provide an alternative Docker apt repository. Ignoring may cause install failure.
